methods.description: Process for extracting acid-insoluble organic-walled microfossils (palynomorphs) from shale samples using standard conventional methods involving acid demineralization and maceration, and preparing them for microscopic study.

steps.description: The materials for this study were obtained by sampling the shale intervals of the Enugu Formation. Exposures of the formation were systematically logged at different localities, such as Ozalla Junction at unit Loc. 1 in the southwest, along Enugu-Port Harcourt expressway, up to unit Loc. 9, near first flyover across the Enugu-Onitsha expressway, in the northern part of the study area (see Fig. 3).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain shale samples from systematically logged geological exposures.
inputs: Systematic logging data (localities Loc. 1 to Loc. 9)

steps.description: Each sample was thoroughly cleaned to remove field contaminants.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean shale samples to remove contaminants.

steps.description: 10 g of each shale sample was weighted out using weighing balance...
steps.searchable_summary: Weigh out 10g of each cleaned shale sample.

steps.description: ...and gently crushed with agate mortar and piston.
steps.searchable_summary: Gently crush weighed shale samples with mortar and piston.

steps.description: HCL acid treatment was unnecessary since the shale samples signaled non-calcareous. The crushed calcite-free sample was digested for 72 hours in 48 % hydrofluoric acid for removal of silicates.
steps.searchable_summary: Digest crushed samples in 48% hydrofluoric acid for 72 hours to remove silicates.

steps.description: The samples were diluted with distil water to neutralize the acid...
steps.searchable_summary: Dilute samples with distilled water to neutralize residual acid.

steps.description: ...and then sievewashed through 10 microns nylon mesh to enable the organic residue be free from mud and acid.
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve-wash diluted samples through a 10 micron nylon mesh.

steps.description: The sievewashed 10 g residue equivalent was partitioned into two parts, 5 g each, for oxidation and the other 5 g for kerogen studies.
steps.searchable_summary: Partition the sieve-washed residue into two 5g parts.

steps.description: The 5 g acid-free organic residue extract from shale was oxidized for 30 minutes in 70% conc. nitric acid...
steps.searchable_summary: Oxidize one 5g residue part with 70% nitric acid for 30 minutes.

steps.description: ...and 5 minutes in Schulze solution to render the fossils translucent for transmitted light microscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: Oxidize the same residue part with Schulze solution for 5 minutes.

steps.description: The oxidized residues were rinsed in 2% KOH solution to neutralize the acid.
steps.searchable_summary: Rinse oxidized residue in 2% KOH solution to neutralize acid.

steps.description: Heavy liquid separation using zinc chloride was employed to separate the palynomorphs, and remove resistant coarse mineral particles and undigested organic matter.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform heavy liquid separation using zinc chloride to separate palynomorphs.

steps.description: The palynomorph residues were collected on the sieve...
steps.searchable_summary: Collect palynomorph residues after heavy liquid separation.
inputs: Output from heavy liquid separation (palynomorph fraction)

steps.description: ...and stained with Safranin -O to increase the visibility for microscopic study.
steps.searchable_summary: Stain palynomorph residue with Safranin-O.

steps.description: Aliquot s were dispersed with polyvinyl alcohol, dried on cover-slips and mounted in petro-poxy resin.
steps.searchable_summary: Disperse aliquots with PVA, dry on cover-slips, and mount in petro-poxy resin.

steps.description: One slide was made from each sample for transmitted light microscopic study.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare one slide from each sample.
outputs: Set of prepared slides (one per sample)

methods.description: Process for examining prepared palynomorph slides under transmitted light microscopy to identify, count, and analyze the distribution and abundance of different palynomorph species and groups.

steps.description: One slide was made from each sample for transmitted light microscopic study.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine prepared palynomorph slides using transmitted light microscopy.

steps.description: Terrigenous species such as fern spores were the most abundant in almost all the examined samples, followed by pollen species of mangrove affinity. Marine species such as dinoflagellate cysts recorded very few presence.The peridiniacean species with a proximate cyst affinity predominates over the gonyaulacaceans. Terrigenous species Spores: Among the terrigenous species, spores were themost abundant and less diverse. Among the fern spores, Laevigatosporitesovatus was the most abundant (58 counts) in sample En/L9/S10, (Table 1) (Fig. 4). The samples from Loc. 1, 2a, 5, 7, and 8, recorded higher percentage frequency of spores (Table 3)... Fresh water species, such as Azolla cretacea, were also recorded... Pollen: Pollen gains were the second most abundant in all the samples but were the most diverse... Among the pollen grains, Longapertites marginatus produced the highest counts (30) in sample En/L4/S5 (Fig. 4).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify different palynomorph species and groups on the slides.
outputs: Identified palynomorph species list per sample

steps.description: Table 1 shows the absolute occurrence and distribution of palynomorphs present in the samples from Loc. 1 to 9. ... Laevigatosporitesovatus was the most abundant (58 counts) in sample En/L9/S10...
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify the absolute abundance (counts) of each identified palynomorph species per sample.
outputs: Absolute counts of each species per sample

steps.description: The samples from Loc. 1, 2a, 5, 7, and 8, recorded higher percentage frequency of spores (Table 3), with 45% count at Loc. 1, Loc. 2a (71 %), Loc. 5 (57 %), Loc. 7 (70 %), and 83 % at Loc. 8. ... sample OZ/L3/S4 from Loc. 3 recorded higher percentage frequency of pollen count (71 %) followed by sample OZ/L2b/S3 (64 %) and EN/L4/S5 (49 %).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate percentage frequency for palynomorph groups or species in samples.
inputs: Absolute counts of species per sample
outputs: Percentage frequency of palynomorphs per sample/group

steps.description: Table 1 shows the absolute occurrence and distribution of palynomorphs present in the samples from Loc. 1 to 9. ... (Table 3)...
steps.searchable_summary: Record and summarize the occurrence, distribution, absolute counts, and percentage frequency data.
outputs: Data tables summarizing results (e.g., Table 1, Table 3)

steps.description: Terrigenous species such as fern spores were the most abundant... Pollen gains were the second most abundant... Marine species such as dinoflagellate cysts recorded very few presence. The peridiniacean species... predominates over the gonyaulacaceans.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze the relative abundance and diversity of different palynomorph groups and species.
outputs: Analysis of palynomorph assemblage composition (e.g., dominant groups, diversity patterns)
